channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Large cupboard blocking pavement, 9th May
item.description:
A neighbour sighted a large white van dump the cupboard around 9:30pm last night. It is blocking the whole pavement which is dangerous for anyone in a wheelchair or with a buggy as they will either have to go in the road or cross to get around it

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Bracondale Road, Abbey Wood
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Persistent fly tippong, 9th May
item.description:
I am a resident of Strathnairn Street, South Bermondsey and I am writing to raise serious concerns regarding the ongoing fly-tipping and littering issues along Lucey way, on the outskirts of the estate. This is a persistent issue, and completely unacceptable. We are now in the situation where enforcement is so weak, that the waste is cleared during the day, only for the area to be covered in dumped rubbish again by the following morning. The waste is clearly not exclusively household waste, with brazen businesses now taking the opportunity to dump commercial and construction waste, including paint cans, tiles, building materials etc. This suggests to me that businesses are using the area as an unofficial dumping ground, with no consequences. I have lived in the area for 5 years, and frankly the area is the dirtiest I have ever seen it. Residents are being failed by the current approach. I am asking the council to take immediate and enforceable action: 1) installation of CCTV covering the bin area on Lucey way 2) targeted enforcement of repeat offenders 3) increased fines and prosecution 4) a clearly outlined long-term strategy Other councils are taking this issue seriously. For example, in Hackney, £200 fines are issued for the dropping of a singular cigarette. In South Bermondsey, however, there is large scale dumping in almost every estate, with no consequence. This is a social norm here, everyone disposes their large waste at the nearest estate, and it needs to stop.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): St James's Road, Bermondsey, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Chest of drawers, 9th May
item.description:
Outside 32-34 Sutcliffe rd chest of drawers abandoned
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): The Slade, Plumstead
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Complaint about tree work, 9th May
item.description:
Resident has reported this to me as ward cllr:- “It concerns the footpath that runs from Kemnal to Belmont. It has been pretty overgrown (some text removed) “Yesterday 2 men finally turned up to do some work, (not idverde, these were independent tree fellers employed by the council). Some text removed “we both commented how quickly the workers were progressing along the footpath, I estimate they were only there for 20 mins, it was when we came out that we realised why they were so quick. Although the path is certainly improved, the workers seem to have done a pretty shoddy incomplete job. I have attached a few videos to highlight the issues, (I can forward if required) The path is still very narrow in parts where they have not cut back enough, the overhanging branches are still overhanging at a dangerous level (for me at least), and the edges of the path have not been reinstated, some waste has been dumped in the verge and not taken away. To be fair they have done an ok job around the entrance to the school (probably as they knew that would get noticed). “ Can you please investigate this complaint and respond to me by email? Thanks Cllr Alison Stammers
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Woodside Avenue, Chislehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Vandalized LTN camera, 9th May
item.description:
This traffic camera which is supposed to enforce the local LTN has been broken for a good part of a year. I reported this around Christmas and it is still unfixed. It seems to be that there’s no interest in fixing this, but this actually makes the neighborhood safer.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 68, Whittington Road, Muswell Hill, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Dead hedgehog, 9th May
item.description:
There is a dead small hedgehog on the land behind 70 Marden Crescent.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Marden Crescent, Barnehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Untaxed vehicle, 9th May
item.description:
A blue Volkswagen van has been parked on Kimberley Rd for months. It does not appear to have moved at any time. On arrival the vehicle was taxed but this expired on 1 March 2026.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Kimberley Road, Elmers End, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Litter - discarded cans / bottles / plastics, 9th May
item.description:
Previously reported a few months ago. And was informed action will be taken, yet to see this slip road area being swept and cleaned. There is lots of litter on the slip road old cans / plastic bottles and empty crisp packets. Please arrange to sweep and keep Britain tidy. Thanks
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bronze Age Way, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Long Potholes on Perry St near Alva Glen entrance gates opposite Leas Green near the central island reservation are extremely hazardous for cyclists using Perry St., 9th May
item.description:

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Perry Street, Chislehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Disgusting base of tree turned into urinal, 9th May
item.description:
What on earth is going on here? Someone’s put urinal style pebbles at the bottom of this tree. It’s now disgusting, wet, muddy. Looks like a toilet. What on earth has happened. So disgusting! Please please please can it be cleared up. It can’t be good for the tree.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 69, Lordship Lane, East Dulwich, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pot holes hazardous to cyclists on Perry St near entrance Thompsons Nursery and The Thatch, 9th May
item.description:
Pot holes hazardous to cyclists on Perry St near entrance Thompsons Nursery and The Thatch
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Perry Street, Chislehurst, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Mattress and metal waste left on street, 9th May
item.description:
More fly tipping at this location, this time an old mattress, metal grates and other assorted rubbish. Please remove and clean ASAP. Seriously, this happens 4-5x per week - Please install cctv cameras and signs to deter this anti social behaviour.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Benn Street, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet
